CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS STATED A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND THE CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT UNCERTAIN. ALTHOUGH URATA DID NOT ATTACH A COPY OF THE ALLEGED CONTRACT, IT PLED THE ALLEGED CONTRACT'S LEGAL INTENDMENT AND EFFECT, WHICH IS SUFFICIENT. BOSA'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. HOWEVER, THE COURT NOTES THAT ALTHOUGH IT CAN TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE EXISTENCE OF PLEADINGS AND OTHER DOCUMENTS IN THE COURT RECORD, THE COURT CANNOT ACCEPT AS TRUE THE CONTENTS OF PLEADINGS OR EXHIBITS JUST BECAUSE THEY ARE PART OF THE COURT RECORD. DAY V. SHARP (1975) 50 CAL.APP.3D 904, 914. THEREFORE, THE ALLEGED CONTRACT BETWEEN QUORUM AND BOSA THAT IS RELIED ON IN THE MOVING PAPERS IS NOT PROPERLY CONSIDERED ON THIS DEMURRER. Christopher A. Viadro, Esq., from ButlerViadro, LLP, 510-287-2400, for plaintiff Morven Liburn
Disclosure made by Judge Kahn, about knowing Mr. Breall and that he is the brother of Judge Susan Breall, but he thinks that none of that affects his ability to be fair and impartial; no objections made.
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS DEFENDANT BOSA DEVELOPMENT CALIFORNIA II, INC. DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled with 10 days leave to answer. Cross-Complainant has stated a cause of action for breach of contract and the cause of action is not uncertain. Although Urata did not attach a copy of the alleged contract, it pled the alleged contract's legal intendment and effect, which is sufficient. Bosa's Request for Judicial Notice is granted. However, the Court notes that although it can take judicial notice of the existence of pleadings and other documents in the court record, the Court cannot accept as true the contents of pleadings or exhibits just because they are part of the court record. Day v. Sharp (1975) 50 Cal.App.3d 904, 914. Therefore, the alleged contract between Quorum and Bosa that is relied on in the moving papers is not properly considered on this demurrer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077
